mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding real economic activity, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. This is an always-on trend signal that does not require a threshold trigger.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO level change vs forward 60-day returns is below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in expansions as defined by NBER.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of at least 0.3 percentage points signals a rapidly tightening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This is a threshold-triggered signal activated by a large one-month drop.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases by 0.3pp or more from previous month
failure_condition: After trigger events, median forward 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p>0.05) or the frequency of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Combined evidence of very low short-term interest rates (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%) and year-over-year growth in retail sales (RSAFS > 2%) indicates that accommodative monetary policy is successfully stimulating consumer demand. These jointly produce a strong positive signal for S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days, as earnings growth expectations rise.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the forward 20-day return is negative more than 40% of instances, or the average return does not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 100 bps annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of residential investment and broader economic strength. In a post-recession recovery regime, rising housing starts reliably predict positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as the economy gains momentum.
failure_condition: During periods identified as post-recession by NBER, the correlation between HOUST level change and forward 60-day returns is not positive, or the signal's hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) at extremely low levels (near zero) signals maximal monetary accommodation, compressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations. This is an always-on positive signal over a very short 5-day horizon as the market prices in the sustained ease.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, the 5-day forward return distribution is not significantly shifted above zero (KS-test p>0.05) relative to periods when FEDFUNDS > 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month gains in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS > 0.2%) and industrial production (INDPRO > 0.1%) provide a powerful joint signal of synchronized economic expansion. This interaction-driven signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as both labor demand and output confirm growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.2% and INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.1%
failure_condition: When both conditions hold simultaneously, the median 20-day S&P 500 return is not positive, or the signal's information coefficient (IC) is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 3% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, which raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and depresses stock prices. This threshold-triggered signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following CPI yoy > 3% events, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative, or the percentage of negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year growth in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 5% suggests consumption is unsustainably high relative to income trends, raising the risk of mean reversion and negative earnings surprises. This threshold-triggered overshoot signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: After RSAFS yoy > 5% triggers, the median 5-day forward return is not negative, or the signal produces more than 50% positive returns.
